title: House Robber II
hints: The circular adjacency means the first and last houses conflict, unlike a linear row. How can you break this cycle to use standard logic?
Solve two separate linear versions of the problem: one excluding the first house, and one excluding the last. The answer is the maximum of these two results.
For each linear version, use dynamic programming where the state at index i represents the max money robbing up to i. The final answer is the max of the two linear DP results.
